title: How to convert a GEDCOM file to CSV (free, in your browser)

segments.voice: Got a GEDCOM file exported from Ancestry, MyHeritage, or FamilySearch?

scene.headline:
GEDCOM file
on your hands?
scene.sub: exported from Ancestry, MyHeritage, Gramps, FamilySearch

segments.voice: But you want it in a spreadsheet to sort, filter, and check your tree.

scene.headline:
Need it as
a spreadsheet
scene.sub: names, dates, places in sortable rows

segments.voice: Your family tree is private. This converts it without uploading anything.

scene.headline:
Your family data
stays on your device

segments.voice: Free. No signup. Nothing to install.
Go to twineconvert dot com slash gedcom to csv.
Drop the dot ged file. Click convert. Open the CSV in Excel or Sheets.
Link's in the bio, give it a try!
